headline: EWC 2026 Hits Its Stride: Unexpected Victories, Soaring Viewership, and a $75M Prize Pool
dek: As the Esports World Cup continues in Paris, new champions are emerging and viewership records are shattering for several major tournaments.
body_markdown:
The Esports World Cup 2026 is officially roaring through Paris, transforming the Paris Expo Porte de Versailles into a vibrant arena for digital gladiators. Opened on July 8 and set to conclude on August 23, this massive event has gathered over 2,000 elite players from 200 leading esports clubs, representing more than 100 countries. With 25 tournaments spanning 24 popular games, the EWC 2026 boasts a staggering $75 million total prize pool, setting a new benchmark for competitive gaming.

Currently, the spotlight shines on the 'FC Pro World Championship' for EA Sports FC 26. This high-stakes football simulation tournament is underway from July 22 to July 26, challenging 36 players in pursuit of a $1,500,000 prize. The initial League Stage, which ran from July 22-24, saw 36 competitors battling it out, with the top 24 advancing to the intense Playoff rounds.

Beyond the pitch, other titles are carving out their own narratives of success and surging popularity. League of Legends, for instance, has ignited its second week at the EWC 2026 with an electrifying series of surprise results and, according to reports, significantly stronger audience numbers. This surge suggests a growing, engaged fanbase tuning in for the unforeseen twists and turns of the competition.

Similarly, Garena's Free Fire is reportedly experiencing a remarkable moment at the EWC 2026, with its event becoming its most popular in four years. This reported jump in viewership and engagement signals a renewed and robust interest in the mobile battle royale, reaching heights not seen in a long time.

While some tournaments are just hitting their stride, others have already concluded. The Dota 2 tournament, for example, ran from July 7-19, featuring 24 teams and a formidable $2 million prize pool. Team Spirit was the 2025 champion for Dota 2, highlighting the intense strategic gameplay that makes the title a consistent draw in the esports landscape.

The Esports World Cup 2026 is not just a collection of tournaments; it's a dynamic showcase of competitive gaming's expanding reach and undeniable appeal. As surprising victories unfold and viewership figures climb, the event solidifies its position as a major force in the global esports calendar.

sources.title: Esports World Cup 2026 Continues in Paris
